title: Oncidium candidum
taxonomy: Oncidium candidum is a type of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. Within this family, Oncidium candidum is classified under the genus Oncidium, which includes approximately 300 species. The genus Oncidium is further divided into several subgenera, with Oncidium candidum falling under the subgenus Oncidiinae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and its evolutionary history.

intro: Oncidium candidum, also known as the White Oncidium, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with traditional uses including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Oncidium candidum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Oncidium candidum is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to relieve respiratory symptoms and promote overall well-being.
